evals.name: what-is-para-in-this-os-narrative-mode
evals.prompt: what is PARA in this OS?
evals.expected_output: Skill triggers. Reads README.md §'What is PARA in this OS?' (or §'Project Map') + locked decision #1 in system-design.md §7 + Configuration tokens from CLAUDE.md. Answers with the 5-folder workspace layout (0-Inbox/, 1-Projects/, 2-Coding/, 3-Resources/, 4-Archive/), explicitly distinguishes from USER.md's 6-folder Mac Desktop PARA, rejects generic Tiago-Forte theory with a one-line redirect. Every factual claim has *(source: path:line)* citation. Narrative mode (no structured key:value:source block).
preconditions: README.md exists with §'What is PARA in this OS?' section (added 2026-05-17 in Pass 2h item 15r).
system-design.md §7 decision #1 covers the 5-folder PARA layout.
CLAUDE.md Configuration section is filled in (setup_completed present).

assertions.text: Output names exactly five workspace folders (0-Inbox, 1-Projects, 2-Coding, 3-Resources, 4-Archive)
Output cites README.md and/or system-design.md with *(source: path:line)* format
Output explicitly notes Mac Desktop PARA is a separate system (6-folder)
Output does NOT lecture on generic Tiago-Forte PARA theory beyond a one-line redirect
Output does not embed paths as raw strings — uses Configuration-resolved paths or token references

evals.name: what-is-workspace-projects-token
evals.prompt: what is workspace.projects in this OS?
evals.expected_output: Skill triggers. Reads Configuration section of CLAUDE.md (§Configuration, lines ~17-58 or current location). Returns the literal value (1-Projects) + resolved path (beru-workspace/1-Projects) + cites CLAUDE.md with line range. May surface related tokens (workspace.root, workspace.inbox, workspace.archive). Re-reads Configuration at invocation — does NOT cache.
preconditions: CLAUDE.md Configuration section exists with workspace.projects = 1-Projects.

assertions.text: Output names workspace.projects value as '1-Projects'
Output cites CLAUDE.md Configuration section with line range
Output mentions the resolved path beru-workspace/1-Projects
Output does NOT hardcode workspace.root as beru-workspace if Configuration uses a different value

evals.name: what-skills-do-i-have-dynamic-glob
evals.prompt: what skills do I have?
evals.expected_output: Skill triggers. Uses Glob/Bash to enumerate .claude/skills/*/SKILL.md at invocation time (NOT hardcoded). Reads frontmatter description: line for each. Groups into categories (lifecycle, design, contacts, briefing, research, CCv4, etc.). Returns a structured list with skill name + one-line description. NEVER lists skills from a hardcoded list in the SKILL.md body.
preconditions: .claude/skills/ exists with 51+ skill subfolders, each containing SKILL.md with description: frontmatter.

assertions.text: Output reflects actual count of skills present in .claude/skills/ (verifiable via glob)
Output groups skills into intuitive categories (not just an alphabetized dump)
Output includes a one-line description per skill (sourced from frontmatter description field)
Output is NOT generated from a hardcoded list — adding a new skill at .claude/skills/test-x/SKILL.md and re-running should surface it

evals.name: what-did-we-decide-about-contacts-location
evals.prompt: what did this OS decide about where contacts live?
evals.expected_output: Skill triggers. Reads system-design.md §7 (starting line 481). Finds decision #21 (Contacts directory location). Returns the locked answer (<workspace.resources>/contacts/<slug>.md, NOT memory/contacts/) with file:line citation. May also surface decision #22 (schema). Explains the rationale briefly (per locked decision text).
preconditions: system-design.md §7 row #21 exists with the contacts directory decision.

assertions.text: Output identifies decision #21 explicitly
Output names the canonical location <workspace.resources>/contacts/<slug>.md (or token-resolved)
Output cites system-design.md with line number
Output explains that contacts/ is in 3-Resources (Reference material) NOT memory/

evals.name: claude-self-consult-via-structured-args
evals.prompt: /os-guide --token workspace.projects
evals.expected_output: Structured mode invocation. Skill detects --token arg (or equivalent structured input). Returns YAML-ish key:value:source triple. Zero prose padding. Includes key, value, source path:line, resolved_path, related_tokens (workspace.root, workspace.inbox, workspace.archive), canonical_files list, last_verified date. Designed for Claude to parse, not for human reading.
preconditions: CLAUDE.md Configuration section filled in.

assertions.text: Output is structured (key:value pairs, YAML-ish) not narrative prose
Output contains 'key:', 'value:', and 'source:' fields at minimum
Output does NOT include narrative voice phrases ('Here's what matters', 'Worth noting')
Output includes resolved_path with Configuration tokens substituted

evals.name: how-do-i-connect-slack-procedural
evals.prompt: how do I connect Slack to this OS?
evals.expected_output: Skill triggers. Reads TOOLS.md §'How to add a tool' (added 2026-05-17 in Pass 2h item 15r) + the existing TOOLS.md Slack row. Returns numbered steps: declare in .mcp.json, run /mcp, OAuth, verify with a slack_search_channels probe, flip TOOLS.md entry. Cites TOOLS.md. Mentions /os-guide --sync as the last step.
preconditions: TOOLS.md has §'How to add a tool' section (Pass 2h item 15r prerequisite).
Slack entry exists in TOOLS.md.

assertions.text: Output gives numbered procedural steps (not abstract overview)
Output references TOOLS.md §'How to add a tool' or the Slack-specific row
Output mentions /mcp command for OAuth
Output mentions running /os-guide --sync afterward (or notes it for future)

evals.name: what-is-the-rule-about-duplicate-projects
evals.prompt: what's the rule about creating duplicate projects?
evals.expected_output: Skill triggers. Reads SOUL.md §Operating Principles. Identifies 'Connect before create' as the relevant principle. Returns it verbatim or paraphrased lightly with citation. May also surface 'Consolidate before duplicate'.
preconditions: SOUL.md exists with Operating Principles section (6 principles).

assertions.text: Output identifies 'Connect before create' principle (or 'Consolidate before duplicate')
Output cites SOUL.md
Output quotes or paraphrases the principle text accurately

evals.name: topic-recall-defers-to-find
evals.prompt: do I have anything on Anthropic admin API?
evals.expected_output: /os-guide does NOT trigger as the primary skill. The query is topic-keyed recall over user content (research notes), which is /find's domain per locked decision #18. If /os-guide is consulted in error, it should respond with a one-line deferral: 'That's topic-keyed recall — use /find for that.'
preconditions: /find skill exists and is the unified recall surface (decision #18).

assertions.text: /find triggers (NOT /os-guide), OR /os-guide explicitly defers to /find in its response
If /os-guide is invoked, it does NOT attempt to search across user content
Output mentions /find as the right skill if deferral happens

evals.name: person-question-defers-to-contact
evals.prompt: who is Omar?
evals.expected_output: /os-guide does NOT trigger. The query is entity-keyed recall about a person, which is /contact's domain per locked decision #21/22. /contact wins via PRECEDENCE clause when the query target matches a file in contacts/.
preconditions: /contact skill exists with the PRECEDENCE rule.
omar-zennadi.md exists in contacts/.

assertions.text: /contact triggers (NOT /os-guide)
Output is about Omar Zennadi, not generic OS structure

evals.name: fresh-fork-setup-defers-to-bootstrap
evals.prompt: I just cloned this repo, set me up
evals.expected_output: /os-guide does NOT trigger. The query is first-run interactive setup, which is /bootstrap's domain. /bootstrap refuses re-runs on configured forks (T2 invariant), so this routes there.
preconditions: /bootstrap skill exists.

assertions.text: /bootstrap triggers (NOT /os-guide)
Output is an interactive setup flow, not a documentation lookup

evals.name: imperative-create-project-defers-to-new-project
evals.prompt: create a new project for Q3 OKR work
evals.expected_output: /os-guide does NOT trigger. The query is imperative action ('create'), not explanatory ('how do I create'). /new-project handles action; /os-guide explains. Disambiguation rule from skill body: imperative form → action skill; question form → /os-guide.
preconditions: /new-project skill exists.

assertions.text: /new-project triggers (NOT /os-guide)
If /os-guide is invoked, it explicitly defers and points at /new-project

evals.name: gap-topic-surfaces-honestly
evals.prompt: why were these 5 MCPs chosen and not others?
evals.expected_output: Skill triggers but maps to a 'Gaps' row (MCP rationale). Surfaces honestly: 'No canonical source documents the full rationale. Closest related: system-design.md decisions #17 (vision), #20 (plugin MCPs), #23 (exa) for partial rationale. If you want to lock the rationale, write it down at <workspace.resources>/reference/mcp-selection-rationale.md and re-ask after /os-guide --sync.' Does NOT improvise an answer.
preconditions: system-design.md §7 has decisions #17, #20, #23 about MCPs.
No mcp-selection-rationale.md exists at <workspace.resources>/reference/.

assertions.text: Output explicitly states 'no canonical source documents this' (or equivalent)
Output points at closest-related canonical files (decisions #17/#20/#23) with citations
Output does NOT improvise a rationale (no fabricated 'we chose these because...')
Output suggests where to write the answer down if user wants to lock it

evals.name: sources-disagree-explicit-surface
evals.prompt: is workspace PARA 5 folders or 6 folders?
evals.expected_output: Skill detects potential contradiction (README.md says 5-folder workspace, USER.md mentions 6-folder Mac Desktop — but these describe DIFFERENT scopes, not contradiction). Skill MUST disambiguate explicitly: 'Workspace PARA is 5 folders (per README.md). Mac Desktop PARA — separate system — is 6 folders (per USER.md). Which one are you asking about?' Asks one disambiguating question via AskUserQuestion if scope unclear.
preconditions: README.md describes 5-folder workspace PARA.
USER.md describes 6-folder Mac Desktop PARA.

assertions.text: Output explicitly disambiguates workspace PARA vs Mac Desktop PARA
Output cites both sources (README.md and USER.md)
Output does NOT silently pick one as 'the answer'

evals.name: generic-para-theory-redirected
evals.prompt: what is PARA?
evals.expected_output: Ambiguous query (could mean generic theory or this-OS implementation). Skill defaults to the this-OS answer per Step 6 (this-OS-specific over generic). May ask one disambiguating question OR answer the this-OS version with a one-line generic-theory redirect: 'PARA is Tiago Forte's organizational framework — see his book or Wikipedia. In this OS specifically, PARA means [5-folder workspace layout].'
preconditions: README.md §'What is PARA in this OS?' exists.

assertions.text: Output prioritizes the this-OS-specific answer over generic theory
Output mentions Tiago Forte and/or Wikipedia ONLY as a one-line redirect, not a lecture
Output gives the this-OS-specific PARA layout (5 folders)

evals.name: generic-claude-code-question-out-of-scope
evals.prompt: what is Claude Code?
evals.expected_output: Out of scope. /os-guide refuses with redirect: 'That's not OS-shaped — it's a question about Claude Code itself. See Anthropic's docs at https://docs.claude.com/claude-code or the official help system.' Does NOT improvise an answer.

assertions.text: Output refuses gracefully (no fabricated description of Claude Code)
Output points at Anthropic's external docs
Output does NOT lecture on what Claude Code is

evals.name: sync-mode-trigger-explicit
evals.prompt: /os-guide --sync
evals.expected_output: Skill triggers in --sync mode. Phase 1: inventory snapshot (glob skills/brands/contacts/refs/projects/decisions/MCPs/tool-status). Phase 2: diff against last-sync.json (if exists; else first-sync). Phase 3: drift detection (canonical source file existence + section line ranges). Phase 4: Markdown report. Phase 5: AskUserQuestion gate with 4 options (Apply all / Apply some / Skip / Cancel). NEVER auto-applies. NEVER touches files outside .claude/skills/os-guide/.
preconditions: .claude/skills/os-guide/state/ exists (with or without last-sync.json).

assertions.text: Skill detects --sync mode and runs the 5-phase pipeline
Skill produces an inventory report (Markdown) before any mutation
Skill gates ALL routing-table edits behind AskUserQuestion
Skill does NOT modify any file outside .claude/skills/os-guide/
Skill does NOT run git add or git commit

evals.name: sync-mode-trigger-natural-language
evals.prompt: I added a new skill, update /os-guide
evals.expected_output: Natural-language --sync trigger. Skill detects intent and runs --sync mode same as explicit /os-guide --sync.

assertions.text: Skill detects sync intent from natural-language phrasing
Skill runs same 5-phase pipeline as explicit --sync

evals.name: default-mode-makes-no-mutations
evals.prompt: what does this OS do?
evals.expected_output: Default mode is read-only. Skill reads canonical sources, produces narrative answer with citations. Does NOT modify any file. Does NOT run /sync as a side-effect. Pure read-only invocation.

assertions.text: Skill does NOT modify any file in default mode
Skill does NOT auto-run --sync as a side-effect
Output is purely informational (read response)

evals.name: fresh-fork-unfilled-config-graceful-error
evals.prompt: what is workspace.projects?
evals.expected_output: If CLAUDE.md Configuration is empty or placeholder (e.g., setup_completed: missing), skill short-circuits per Step 2: 'Cannot answer — Configuration section in root CLAUDE.md is unfilled or placeholder. This usually means /bootstrap hasn't run yet. Run /bootstrap first, then re-ask.' Does NOT improvise.
preconditions: Simulated state: CLAUDE.md exists but Configuration section is unfilled (this is a fresh-fork scenario).

assertions.text: Output explicitly identifies Configuration as unfilled/placeholder
Output redirects to /bootstrap
Output does NOT improvise a default value

evals.name: missing-canonical-file-graceful-flag
evals.prompt: what is PARA in this OS?
evals.expected_output: If routing table points at README.md but the file is missing or renamed, skill surfaces: 'Routing table points at README.md but file is missing — has it been renamed? Run /os-guide --sync to detect and fix.' Does NOT improvise from memory.
preconditions: Simulated state: README.md temporarily renamed to README-OS.md.

assertions.text: Output surfaces the missing-file failure explicitly
Output suggests /os-guide --sync as the fix
Output does NOT produce a PARA answer from memory
